name: Namibia

colors.symbolism: the clear Namibian sky and the Atlantic Ocean; the country's marine resources and the importance of rain and water
Namibia's most important resource — its people — and their heroism and determination to build a future of equal opportunity
the country's vegetation and agricultural resources
peace and unity (the fimbriations of the diagonal band)
the warmth of the sun and the energy of life (the upper-hoist sun emblem)

description: A red diagonal band running from the lower hoist to the upper fly, fimbriated above and below by narrow white stripes; the upper-hoist triangle is blue, the lower-fly triangle is green. In the upper-hoist (blue) triangle is a golden sun with twelve straight triangular rays.
history: Adopted on 21 March 1990, the day Namibia gained independence from South African administration after a long liberation struggle. The flag was designed by a sub-committee of the Constituent Assembly drawing on more than 870 entries to a national competition; the colours derive from the SWAPO party flag (blue, red and green) with the white fimbriations and golden sun added to give the flag a non-partisan national character.

features.symbolism: a red diagonal band fimbriated in white separates a blue upper-hoist triangle from a green lower-fly triangle: blue for sky and ocean, red for the people of Namibia, green for vegetation, white for peace and unity
life, energy and warmth; reflects the prominent role of the sun in the Namibian landscape
